vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual data. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or entirely subterranean. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; the black pot contains only a singular, thin, etiolated green stem. P3 (Pothos) is absent; the white rabbit anchor is present but the plant is not.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (Only a single, struggling stem remains)

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Contains a single, thin, light-starved stem. High probability of failure.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: White rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent. The soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: No evidence of Silver Guest; soil surface is barren.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or lacking drainage, which may have contributed to the loss of the specimens.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: A metallic object (spoon) appears in the T-4 image and persists in subsequent frames, acting as an uncatalogued intervention. The white rabbit anchor is the only consistent biological marker.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a rapid decline or initial absence of plant life. The transition from T-5 to CURRENT shows the introduction of a spoon in the yellow pot area and the continued absence of the expected foliage.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of photosynthetic material suggests the plants have either been removed or have suffered total senescence/decay. The remaining stem in the black pot is severely etiolated.
anomalies: Introduction of a metal spoon in the yellow pot (T-4 to CURRENT).
Total absence of Pothos (P3) despite the presence of the scale anchor.
Persistent lack of foliage across all pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a near-total loss of the botanical collection. The environment is currently devoid of healthy plant life. The presence of the white rabbit anchor without the Pothos suggests a disruption in the biome's maintenance. The single stem in the black pot is the only remaining biological evidence, and it is in poor condition. The introduction of the spoon suggests human intervention, possibly for soil aeration or moisture testing, but it has not resulted in visible plant recovery.
